fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue and high analyst estimates are offset by current unprofitability and high valuation multiples.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current FY 441.7% — significant future growth expected.
Forward P/E 38.91 with PEG ratio 0.78 suggests growth may justify valuation.
Current ratio 3.83 and Quick ratio 2.85 indicate strong short-term liquidity.
Debt/Equity 25.36 is high but manageable given cash reserves.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6% and Profit margin -8.5% — the company is currently unprofitable.
Return on equity -6.1% and Return on assets -3.3% show poor returns on capital.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-445.58M — significant cash burn.
Price/Sales (TTM) 29.39 is very high, indicating a premium valuation.
Mean analyst target of $151.30 is below the current spot price.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) has seen significant positive momentum, with shares climbing 17% on June 2nd alone and a remarkable 439% year-to-date. This surge is fueled by strong demand from data centers and AI computing, a recent large upgrade deal with Mediacom, and bullish analyst sentiment. While the company is still working towards profitability, its revenue growth and analyst growth estimates are exceptionally high.
